title: Indoor Voice
description: Write a program in Python that prompts the user for input and then outputs that same input in lowercase. Assume the user's input will be in English.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to call input() to read user input
Calling str.lower() on the class instead of an instance
Not understanding that lower() returns a new string, it does not modify in place

title: Playback Speed
description: Write a program that prompts the user for input and then outputs that same input, replacing each space with ... (three periods).
common_mistakes: Replacing only the first space using str.replace with a count argument
Forgetting that replace() returns a new string

title: Making Faces
description: Implement a function called convert that accepts a str as input and returns that same input with :) converted to the emoji and :( converted to the emoji. Then, in the same file, implement a function called main that prompts the user for a string of text, calls convert on that input, and prints the result.
common_mistakes: Not defining both main() and convert() as separate functions
Forgetting to call main() at the bottom of the file

title: Deep Thought
description:
According to the Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, the answer to the Great Question of Life, the Universe and Everything is 42. Write a program that prompts the user for the answer to the Great Question. If the answer is 42 (or "forty-two" or "forty two"), output "Yes, that's the answer!". Otherwise output "No".

**Example:**
```
What is the Answer to the Great Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything? 42
Yes, that's the answer!
```
common_mistakes: Not handling the string variants (forty-two, forty two)

title: Home Federal Savings Bank
description:
Prompt the user for a greeting. If it starts with "hello" (case-insensitive), output $0. If it starts with an "h" but not "hello", output $20. Otherwise output $100.

**Example:**
```
Greeting: Hello, Newman
$0
```
common_mistakes: Not stripping leading whitespace before checking the greeting
Using startswith incorrectly without lowercasing first

title: File Extensions
description: Prompt the user for the name of a file and output that file's media type based on the extension. Handle: .gif → image/gif, .jpg/.jpeg → image/jpeg, .png → image/png, .pdf → application/pdf, .txt → text/plain, .zip → application/zip. Anything else outputs application/octet-stream.
common_mistakes: Not lowercasing the extension before comparison
